full_name: Steels for general structural purposes (Allgemeine Baustaehle)
scope_summary: Former German standard specifying non-alloy general structural steels (grades St33, St37-2, USt37-2, RSt37-2, St37-3, St44, St52-3, etc.) for buildings, bridges and load-bearing structures. Superseded by EN 10025 (adopted 1994; St37-2 discontinued 2004, new designation S235JR).

full_name: Hot rolled products of structural steels
scope_summary: European standard (published as DIN EN in Germany) giving technical delivery conditions for hot-rolled structural steel products. Part 1 general conditions; Part 2 non-alloy structural steels (S235, S275, S355); Part 3 normalized fine-grain; Part 4 thermomechanical fine-grain; Part 5 weathering steels; Part 6 quenched-and-tempered high-yield flats. Beuth Verlag holds exclusive sale rights.

European stainless steel standard published as DIN EN in Germany. Part 1 lists stainless steels and chemical compositions, subdivided into corrosion-resisting, heat-resisting and creep-resisting steels; Part 2 sheet/plate/strip for general purposes; Part 3 semi-finished/bars/rods/wire/sections; Parts 4-5 construction-purpose products. Full grade tables are sold by Beuth Verlag.

full_name: Seamless tubes of heat-resistant steels (Nahtlose Rohre aus warmfesten Staehlen)
scope_summary: Former German standard for seamless tubes of heat-resistant steels used in boilers, pipelines, pressure vessels and equipment for service up to ~600 C at high pressure. Common grades St35.8, St45.8, 15Mo3, 13CrMo44, 10CrMo910. Largely replaced by EN 10216-2.

Former German stainless steel delivery-conditions standard covering hot/cold rolled strip and plate, wire rod, drawn wire, bars, forgings and semi-finished products. The greater part of the 1996 edition was superseded by DIN EN 10088, DIN EN 10028-7, DIN EN 10222 and DIN EN 10272; the 2001 edition deals only with drawn wire.

full_name: Aluminium alloys (Part 1 wrought alloys; Part 2 casting alloys)
scope_summary: Former German standard for aluminium alloys. Part 2 covered casting alloys for sand, gravity die, pressure die and investment casting. Superseded by European standards - casting alloys by DIN EN 1706 (published June 1998), wrought alloys by EN 573 / EN 485.
